author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
ṭhākura vaiṣṇava⋅pada, avanīra susampada,
śuna bhāi, hañā ekamana
āśraya loiyā bhaje, tāre kṛṣṇa nāhi tyaje,
āra saba mare akāraṇa

vaiṣṇava⋅caraṇa⋅jala, prema⋅bhakti dite bala,
āra keha nahe balavanta
vaiṣṇava⋅caraṇa⋅reṇu, mastake bhūṣaṇa vinu,
āra nāhi bhūṣaṇera anta

tīrtha-jala pavitra guṇe, likhiyāche purāṇe,
se⋅saba bhaktira pravañcana
vaiṣṇavera pādodaka⋅ sama nahe ei saba,
jāte haya vāñchita⋅pūraṇa

vaiṣṇava⋅saṅgete mana, ānandita anukṣaṇa,
sadā haya kṛṣṇa⋅parasaṅga
dīna narottama kānde, hiyā dhairya nāhi bāndhe,
mora daśā kena haila bhaṅga
en_translation:
The feet of the Vaiṣṇava ṭhākura are the great treasure of the earth. Listen, oh brother, with a single mind. One who takes their shelter and worships, Kṛṣṇa never abandons them; all others simply die in vain.

The water from the Vaiṣṇava's feet has the power to bestow prema-bhakti; no one else is so powerful. The dust from the Vaiṣṇava's feet is an ornament for the head; there is no end to such ornaments.

It is written in the Purāṇas that holy water from pilgrimage sites is pure by nature, but all that is merely a deception of devotion. All these cannot compare to the water from the Vaiṣṇava's feet, by which all desires are fulfilled.

In the association of Vaiṣṇavas, the mind is joyful at every moment, and one always experiences the direct association of Kṛṣṇa. Humble Narottama Dāsa Ṭhākura weeps, his heart finds no patience: "Why has my condition become so broken?"
